sections.paragraph:
σεληνιάζομαι (σελήνη; TestSol 10:35 C; Lucian; Vett. Val. 113, 10; Cat. Cod. Astr. VIII/1 p. 199, 7; Manetho, Apotel. 4, 81; 217, in both cases the act. as v.l. Prim. ‘to be moonstruck’) to experience epileptic seizures, be an epileptic (in the ancient world epileptic seizure was associated with transcendent powers of the moon; cp. Cat. Cod. Astr. IX/2 p. 156, 10f πρὸς ‹δὲ› δαιμονιζομένους, ἐπιληπτικοὺς καὶ σεληνιαζομένους) Mt 17:15. W. δαιμονίζεσθαι 4:24.—RE IV 412, 25ff; BHHW II 1236.—DELG and M-M s.v. σελήνη.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Manetho Manetho , hist., III b.c.—List 5

act. act. = active

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

RE RE = Realencyclopädie für protestantische Theologie und Kirche—List 6

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 919
